name: Caesar Salad Dressing III
directions: Mix in blender and refrigerate.

name: Creamy Caesar Dressing
directions: Toss everything into a blender and blend till smooth.

similar.name: Olive Oil and Vinegar Salad Dressing

name: Anchovy Dressing
directions: Put everything except the olive oil into a food processor.
Slowly drizzle in the olive oil into the running food processor.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.

name: Creamy Dijon Parmesan Dressing
directions: Whirl all the ingredients together in a food processor until smooth.
Use as a dip or salad dressing.

similar.name: Cracked Peppercorn Dressing from the Gourmet Room

name: Cracked Peppercorn Dressing from the Gourmet Room
directions: In a blender or with a whisk, combine the mayonnaise, garlic, parmesan cheese, peppercorns, lemon juice and Tabasco sauce.
Slowly blend in the buttermilk until well combined. Chill until ready to use.

name: Caesar Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a blender.
Blend on lowest speed for 20 seconds to incorporate oil.
Blend on high for 20 more seconds to mix thoroughly.
Place in covered container and refrigerate.

You may not have to mash the anchovies, I did because I was not sure if they would blend properly. Also you could use anchovy paste. With the vinegar and lemon juice I expect this should be good for a couple of weeks if kept in a very cold fridge.

similar.name: Olive Oil and Vinegar Salad Dressing

name: The Creamiest Pepper Parmesan Dressing
directions: In small bowl, combine sour cream, mayonnaise and Parmesan cheese and set aside.
Mash garlic and salt together until it forms a paste.
Add to sour cream mixture along with black pepper.
Whisk in remaining ingredients.
Store covered, in refrigerator.

similar.name: Cracked Peppercorn Dressing from the Gourmet Room

name: Garlic Anchovy Salad Dressing
directions: Place the first nine ingredient in a blender, cover and process until smooth.
While processing, gradually add oil in a steady stream.
Transfer to a jar, cover and refrigerate until serving time.

similar.name: Tangy Blue Cheese Dip and Dressing

name: Sullivan's Steakhouse House Iceberg Wedge
directions: For the Dressing: Place 7 oz. blue cheese, mayonnaise, buttermilk, vinegar, sour cream, oil, sugar, garlic and pepper in a bowl. Using a hand-held mixer on low speed, combine all the ingredients, scraping down the bowl once.
Refrigerate 24 hours or as long as 3 days.
For the Salad: Cut the lettuce head in half. Then cut each half in thirds (if small, cut each half in half).
Trim the core from each wedge.
Place the wedges on chilled salad plates. Spoon about 1/3 cup of dressing over each wedge, allowing it to run over the sides. Sprinkle each with blue cheese and tomatoes, dividing evenly.
Serve chilled.

similar.name: Tangy Blue Cheese Dip and Dressing

name: Tangy Blue Cheese Dip and Dressing
directions: Drop clove of garlic into blender or food processor while the motor is running.
Turn off the blender and add the remainder of the ingredients.
Pulse ingredients together until blended, scraping down the sides once or twice. Do not overblend, you want a few little chunks left for texture.
Thin with a bit of milk if desired.

name: Real Caesar Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients in a food processor and mix until well blended. Use dressing over fresh Romaine greens and garlic croutons.

name: Dry Ranch Salad Dressing Mix
directions: Combine all ingredients in the container of a food processor or blender and process on high speed until well blended and powdery smooth.
Store in air-tight container.
To use mix: Combine 1 tablespoon dry mix with 1 cup milk and 1 cup mayonnaise, mix well. Makes 2 cups.

name: Yogurt Sesame Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients and mix well.
Store in refrigerator.

similar.name: Tangy Blue Cheese Dip and Dressing
Olive Oil and Vinegar Salad Dressing

name: Tangy Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Mash garlic and salt together for dressing.  Place in bowl.
Add Parmesan, lemon juice, mayonnaise, anchovy paste, Dijon, Worcestershire sauce and Tabasco.  Stir until blended.
Whisk in oil in a slow, steady stream to form an emulsion.  Season with salt and pepper, chill.
Toss with romaine lettuce and top with extra Parmesan cheese.

name: Grandpa's Perfect Caesar Dressing
directions: Place in blender or use blender stick.
Blend until smooth.
Serve.

name: Lemon-Feta Dressing
directions: Make the dressing: Combine all ingredients in a blender or food processor and purée until the mixture reaches a smooth and pourable consistency -- about 2 to 3 minutes. Store covered and refrigerated for up to 2 days.

similar.name: Tangy Blue Cheese Dip and Dressing

name: Traditional Caesar Dressing
directions: start by mixing the eggs, garlic, and parmesan.
Once the parmesan is well incorporated, add worcestershire, lemon juice and creole mustard and blend well again.
Continue blending while drizzling in the olive oil.
season to taste with salt and pepper.
Simple, straight forward and delicious!

name: Italian Salad Dressing Mix
directions: Mix all the ingredients together.
Store in an air-tight container in the fridge.

name: Growlies Parmesan Peppercorn Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a small bowl, stirring with a wire whick until smooth.  Cover and chill.

similar.name: Cracked Peppercorn Dressing from the Gourmet Room

name: Spinach Salad With Cranberry and Maple Vinaigrette
directions: Wash and dry the spinach leaves.  Place on a large serving platter.  Sprinkle the pecans, cranberries, and onion rings on top of spinach.
Place the vinegar, cranberry juice, maple syrup, Dijon mustard, salt and pepper in a jar with a tight-fitting lid.  Shake or whisk until well combined.
Add the vegetable oil and shake again.  Just before serving, shake the vinaigrette once more and pour it over the salad.

name: Too Cheesy Garlic Caesar Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients for the dressing in a bowl and mix thoroughly and allow to sit in the refrigerator for one hour before serving.

name: Low-Salt Hidden Valley Ranch Mix
directions: Crumble saltines into a blender. Add onion flakes and parsley. Pulse until the mixture is a fine powder.
Pour the mixture into a bowl. Add the rest of the ingredients and mix well.
Store in an airtight container.
To make salad dressing, add 1 1/2 to 2 tablespoons mix to 1 cup mayo and 1 cup buttermilk. Mix well.
To make dip, add 2 to 3 tablespoons mix to one 16 oz container of sour cream. Mix well.

similar.name: Cracked Peppercorn Dressing from the Gourmet Room

name: Ranch Dressing Mix (Dry)
directions: In a food processor or blender, combine all ingredients and process on high speed until well blended and powdery smooth.
To use mix:
Combine 2 Tablespoon dry mix with 1 cup whole milk (or fresh buttermilk) and 1 cup Light Mayonnaise. Mix well.
Chill for at least 1 hour to let flavors meld.
Recipe yields about 6 Tablespoons of dry mix; OR about 6 cups of dressing (when mixed with milk and mayonnaise).

name: Parsley Buttermilk and Parmesan Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients except Parmesan in a blender.
Cover and run 1 minute until smooth and well blended.
Add Parmesan and run for 30 seconds more.

name: Orange Tahini Dressing
directions: Combine all of the ingredients in a mixing bowl and whisk until smooth.

name: Light Blue Cheese Dressing
directions: In a small bowl ,whisk together the yogurt, milk, mayo, lemon juice, worcestershire sauce and cayenne until its smooth. Stir in the blue cheese and salt and pepper. Cover and refrigerate overnight so the flavors develop.

similar.name: Tangy Blue Cheese Dip and Dressing

name: Ranch Mix
directions: Combine all ingredients and mix well.
To make ranch dip, combine 1 tbsp of mix with 1/2 cup sour cream.
To make ranch salad dressing, combine 1 tbsp of mix with 1/4 cup buttermilk and 1/4 cup mayonnaise.

name: Olive Oil and Vinegar Salad Dressing
directions: Combine egg, garlic, and oil; beat with rotary beater until thick.
Blend in cheese, salt, and cayenne.
Add vinegar to taste; mix well.
Serve with Romaine lettuce and croutons.
